Description.description: Single debit or credit line of a journal entry. Each line affects one account on one entity. Debits and credits across the parent JE must balance; enforced by PostJournalEntryPlugin (Phase 6).
Unique identifier of the user who created the record.
Date and time when the record was created.
Unique identifier of the delegate user who created the record.
Sequence number of the import that created this record.
Unique identifier of the user who modified the record.
Date and time when the record was modified.
Unique identifier of the delegate user who modified the record.
Unique identifier for the organization
Date and time that the record was migrated.
Account this line debits or credits. Plugin validates rm_isactive=true and entity match with the header on save.
Optional cost-center / department code. Reserved for future analytics expansion; not validated against a master in Phase 5.
Credit amount for this line. Exactly one of rm_debit / rm_credit must be non-zero.
Debit amount for this line. Exactly one of rm_debit / rm_credit must be non-zero (memo-only lines are deferred to a future phase).
Denormalized entity from the header for query performance and entity-scoped row-level security. Plugin enforces header.rm_entity = line.rm_entity on save.
Optional line-level external reference (PO number, bill line, invoice line, etc.).
Parent JE header. Deleting a Draft header cascades to lines; posted-JE protection is enforced at the status level in PostJournalEntryPlugin.
Unique identifier for entity instances
Display label for this line, e.g. "Line 1 - Cash - Operating". Plain string in Phase 5; PostJournalEntryPlugin (Phase 6) will compute it as "Line {N} - {AccountName}" on save.
Per-line narrative; appears on the GL detail report.
Position of this line within its JE (1, 2, 3, ...). Enforced contiguous by the plugin.
Optional project code. Reserved for future analytics expansion; not validated against a master in Phase 5.
Status of the Journal Entry Line
Status of the Journal Entry Line
Reason for the status of the Journal Entry Line
Reason for the status of the Journal Entry Line
For internal use only.
Time zone code that was in use when the record was created.
